title: Bikewatching
description: Interactive Mapbox and D3 visualization of Boston-area Bluebikes traffic. Shows Boston and Cambridge bike lanes, station traffic volume by marker size, arrival/departure flow by marker color, and a time-of-day slider for filtering trips.

title: Yolodex (OpenAI Codex Hackathon Winner)
description: A game vision factory that turns any gameplay video into a trained YOLO model with one prompt in minutes. Architected image labeling with parallel subagents, synthetic augmentation, and an autonomous training loop — all packaged as a Codex Skill. Pitched to Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, and Sequoia partners; featured on OpenAI socials (900k+ views, 7k+ reactions).

title: Opal (NexHacks)
description: An AI gaming teammate that joins Discord VC and plays alongside users — 150K+ views across socials. Designed a harness separating macro strategy (every 1-2s) from micro execution (30fps), enabling game-agnostic AI across CS:GO, TFT, and more. Trained custom YOLOv8 for 60fps enemy detection and cut voice latency from 800ms to 400ms.

title: VIMA (HackTech 2x Winner)
description: Visual intelligence for multimodal annotation from egocentric construction footage. Built an auditable spatial memory pipeline that turns hardhat-style video into CII productivity labels, object detections, semantic boxes, masks, depth estimates, spatial zones, and event episodes with cited retrieval. Shipped a hosted dashboard, FastAPI backend, CLI, and MCP server; memory-augmented reasoning improved spatial QA from 0.600 to 0.792 across demo questions.

title: Evolve (Anthropic Builder Hackathon Winner)
description: An interactive timeline generator that builds chronological summaries of research topics using retrieval-augmented LLM pipelines and the Semantic Scholar Paper API. Built a multi-stage Express backend with dynamic year-binning, custom score ranking, and Google Search grounding across 5k+ papers. Polished galaxy-themed UI built with Three.js and Framer Motion, deployed on Vercel.

title: Fulcrum Adventures
description: Mobile app built with React Native and Expo for a nonprofit client at Triton Software Engineering. Led a team of 8 developers building v2 with a MongoDB/Express backend and a Vite + React admin portal.

title: Clarifai
description: Multi-agent system for explaining research topics in video. Finalist at the NVIDIA Agent Hackathon.

title: Clarity
description: A computer vision based pill classifier. Winner of the UCSD HealthLink hackathon.

title: 3dhands
description: Pipeline for tracking 3D hand positions from iPhone video footage using MediaPipe, ARKit camera data, and Pi3X. Extracts 21 joint positions per hand in world-space coordinates with orientation and confidence metrics.

title: LOL Analysis
description: Analysis of over 10,000 pro League of Legends matches to measure how a 15-minute gold lead impacts win rate. Used scikit-learn (Logistic Regression, Random Forest), permutation testing, and grid search to reach ~75% predictive accuracy.

title: Music Recommendation
description: Flask-based music recommendation web app using the Spotify API. Includes a custom recommendation engine and a simple frontend for exploring suggested songs.

title: Anki Card Creator (DiamondHacks 2025)
description: Chrome extension that auto-generates Anki flashcards from web page content. Built for DiamondHacks 2025 using OpenRouter for AI-powered card generation and AnkiConnect for direct import into Anki.

title: SpayLA Website
description: Full-stack TypeScript website built at Triton Software Engineering for SpayLA, a nonprofit focused on spay/neuter services. Deployed on Vercel with concurrent frontend/backend dev servers.
